From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

International lawn bowl competition

This article is about the event organised by the World Bowls Tour. For the event organised by World Bowls & the IIBC, see [World Bowls Indoor Championships](/wiki/World_Bowls_Indoor_Championships "World Bowls Indoor Championships").

The **World Indoor Bowls Championship** is an international [bowls](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") competition held annually at [Potters Resorts](/wiki/Potters_Resorts "Potters Resorts") in [Hopton on Sea](/wiki/Hopton_on_Sea "Hopton on Sea").

In recent history matches are streamed live on YouTube & Facebook, attracting around four million views annually.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The last week of the competition is televised live on [BBC](/wiki/BBC "BBC") where the open singles and open pairs', women's singles and mixed pairs' finals are shown.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=World_Indoor_Bowls_Championships&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The competition was first held in 1979 in  [Coatbridge, Scotland](/wiki/Coatbridge "Coatbridge") as a men's singles only event. The first event was sponsored by [Embassy Cigarettes](/wiki/Embassy_(cigarette) "Embassy (cigarette)").

In 1986, the men's pairs competition was added to the championship.[[3]](#cite_note-telegraph-3) In 1988, a rule change allowed women to compete for the first time and the women's singles competition was created. This rule change also changed expanded the men's singles and pairs competition and both become  [open](/wiki/Open_(sport) "Open (sport)") tournaments.

In 1989, the championships moved to the Guild Hall in  [Preston, England](/wiki/Preston,_Lancashire "Preston, Lancashire") and [Churchill Insurance](/wiki/Churchill_Insurance "Churchill Insurance") took over the sponsorship. While the competition was being held in Preston, [Midland Bank](/wiki/Midland_Bank "Midland Bank") and [SAGA](/wiki/Saga_plc "Saga plc") were also sponsors at various times.

In 1999, [Potters Holidays](/wiki/Potters_Resort "Potters Resort") took over the sponsorship and, as part of the agreement, the event moved to its current home at the company's resort in Hopton-on-Sea.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The mixed pairs competition was added to the championship in 2004.
